summary: Verified Europe/Rome timezone fix for date_from/date_to filters across clienti, leads, analytics, audit + Excel export. 17/17 tests passed including the critical mezzanotte edge case (cliente con created_at 22:30 UTC compare in filtro Roma del giorno successivo).

minor.issue: Lines 204-215 of routes/analytics.py STILL use the OLD UTC-fixed logic (datetime.fromisoformat(...).replace(hour=0,...tzinfo=timezone.utc)). The review_request states '4 punti aggiornati' in analytics.py but this supervisor/unit endpoint was missed. Same Roma-vs-UTC bug remains here for supervisors filtering their unit analytics.
Returns HTTP 500 on invalid date param (data_da='not-a-date') instead of 400. Pre-existing bug, not related to the timezone fix but surfaced during testing.

action_items: Apply rome_date_to_utc_range also to /api/analytics/supervisor/unit (routes/analytics.py lines 204-215) — currently still using UTC-fixed logic and will misfilter Rome local dates near midnight UTC.
Optional: fix /api/analytics/pivot data_da/data_a validation to return 400 instead of 500 on bad input.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/analytics.py:204-215 — supervisor/unit endpoint NOT migrated to rome_date_to_utc_range. Inconsistent with the rest of the analytics module which uses the helper (lines 80-93, 387-400, 597-612).
Helper rome_date_to_utc_range is well-defined and DST-aware (verified for CEST summer, CET winter, AND the DST-transition day 2026-03-29).
datetime.fromisoformat in helper accepts more than YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., full ISO datetimes); minor robustness concern but not security-critical.
seed_data_creation: Insert 2 test clienti in MongoDB with id prefix TEST_TZ_ (auto-cleaned by fixture): one with created_at=2026-08-14T22:30:00Z (CEST edge case), one with created_at=2026-02-14T23:30:00Z (CET edge case). Uses common dummy commessa_id/sub_agenzia_id/created_by.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_timezone_rome.py covers: helper unit tests (CEST/CET/DST transition), /api/clienti?date_from/date_to edge case mezzanotte Roma (PASS), /api/clienti/export/excel filtering (PASS), /api/leads date filter (PASS), /api/analytics/pivot date filter (PASS), /api/audit/sub-agenzia-status-changes regression (PASS). The seed fixture is session-scoped and auto-cleans. ONE OPEN GAP: /api/analytics/supervisor/unit still uses old UTC-fixed logic — main agent must apply rome_date_to_utc_range here too.
rca of the issue: Timezone bug RCA: Old code used datetime.fromisoformat(date_str).replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc), treating the user-input date as UTC midnight. Italian users in CEST (+02:00) see local midnight at 22:00 UTC of the previous day, so a cliente created at 23:30 UTC on day N actually belongs to day N+1 in Roma but was filtered as day N. Fix: rome_date_to_utc_range builds [00:00 Roma, 23:59:59.999999 Roma] using zoneinfo('Europe/Rome') and converts both bounds to UTC via astimezone(timezone.utc). DST is handled automatically by zoneinfo. Verified empirically: cliente with created_at=2026-08-14T22:30:00Z (=2026-08-15 00:30 Rome CEST) now appears ONLY when filtering date_from=2026-08-15 (not 2026-08-14).
